text:
BOTTOM LINE — AI trade collapsing, broken tape character.  
SPY -1.23% and QQQ -1.90% both below the 200-day MA, triggering CTA sell programs, as the AI unwind from MAGS -4.63% and SOXX -0.77% slammed large caps while oil +5.93% war-spike crushed discretionary XLY -4.61% — a stark negative-sum rotation into defense and freight.

INDICES — VIX 18.7 (+5.12%). Best to worst: IWM -0.58%, DIA -1.00%, SPY -1.23%, QQQ -1.90%, ^SOX -0.77%. Small > large by 0.65pp. Cross-asset: Oil +5.93%. SPY and QQQ both below 200-day MA.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 1.1x (price down = distribution), QQQ vol_ratio 1.0x (neutral). Elevated volume in XLC 2.0x and XLY 1.7x confirms heavy selling in comms and discretionary, while XLV 0.7x shows investors ignoring healthcare.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLI Industrials +1.73%, XLV Healthcare +1.26%, XLU Utilities +0.57%, XLE Energy +0.30%, XLRE Real Estate -0.13%, XLF Financials -0.39%, XLK Technology -1.01%, XLB Materials -1.04%, XLP Staples -1.39%, XLC Comms -3.50%, XLY Discretionary -4.61%. Top industries: ITA Defense +3.08%, PAVE Infrastructure +1.61%. Bottom: MAGS -4.63%, SLV -3.45%. Money flowing into defense/freight, out of AI/consumer. Rotation state: AI hardware fading / high-to-low rotation.

POSITIONING — SPY put-call ratio 1.19 (elevated protection demand), QQQ 0.91 (neutral). VIX 18.7 above its 20-day MA of 16.8 signals rising fear. MOVE 80.1 above 70.2 MA shows bond vol stress.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil +5.93% war premium hitting discretionary XLY -4.61% and dragging down airlines and consumer names, while defense LMT +10.54% surged. Gold -2.00% falling despite geopolitical tension — unusual, likely dollar +0.39% crowding it out.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 59.4 Neutral. Top movers: LMT +10.54% (Defense), TMO +8.71% (Medtech), UNP +4.02% (Freight). 20d momentum best: XLE Energy +9.03%, worst: XLY Discretionary -4.40%. Vol spike pick: UNP 3.3x vol + price up = accumulation; TSLA 3.0x vol + price down = distribution. Catalyst: war fears shifting assets. Watch tomorrow: LMT (+10.54%, vol spike + defense momentum); TSLA (-14.52%, below 200-day MA + distribution); UNP (+4.02%, vol spike + freight AI rotation).
